552.reason: [This condition stops scanning at the first double quote, but fails to handle cases where '&' or '|' appear inside string literals after escaped quotes or in multi-segment strings. However, more critically, it does NOT scan beyond the first double quote, so if an illegal '&' or '|' appears AFTER a string (e.g., `printf("hello"); &x;`), it will be missed. But the real bug is: the loop breaks at the FIRST double quote, meaning any code after a string literal on the same line is not checked for type 'a' errors.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition stops scanning at the first double quote, but fails to handle cases where '&' or '|' appear inside string literals after escaped quotes or in multi-segment strings. However, more critically, it does NOT scan beyond the first double quote, so if an illegal '&' or '|' appears AFTER a string (e.g., `printf("hello"); &x;`), it will be missed. But the real bug is: the loop breaks at the FIRST double quote, meaning any code after a string literal on the same line is not checked for type 'a' errors.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition stops scanning at the first double quote, but fails to handle characters after a closing quote or inside character constants (single quotes), potentially missing '&' or '|' outside string literals.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition stops scanning at the first double quote, but lexical errors like single '&' or '|' may appear inside string literals where they are actually allowed; however, more critically, it fails to scan beyond the first string, missing potential errors after the closing quote. But the real issue is: this function scans only up to the first '"' and ignores the rest of the line, so if an illegal '&' or '|' appears after a string literal, it won't be detected. However, the main bug is that the loop condition breaks at the first '"', meaning any characters after a string (including erroneous & or |) are not checked.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition stops scanning at the first double quote, but does not resume after the closing quote, causing characters like '&' or '|' inside string literals to be incorrectly checked as lexical errors.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition stops scanning at the first double quote, but fails to handle cases where '&' or '|' appear inside string literals after escaped quotes or in multi-segment strings; more critically, it ignores characters after a closing quote, potentially missing illegal standalone '&' or '|' outside strings.]
